headline: Sedition Act charges triple against online commentators
context: Attorney General's Chambers filed 36 sedition charges in 2025, predominantly targeting social media users critical of government policies.

cards.big: 36 sedition charges in 2025, up from 12 in 2024
cards.sub: Most charges target social media posts and online commentary

cards.big: Sedition Act 1948 carries up to 3 years imprisonment per offence
cards.sub: Colonial-era law originally designed to suppress anti-British sentiment

cards.big: UN Human Rights Committee urged Malaysia to repeal the Act in 2023
cards.sub: Freedom of expression restrictions deemed disproportionate by 4 UN bodies

cards.big: 28 of 36 charged were individuals under age 35 posting online
cards.sub: Young Malaysians increasingly self-censor on political topics

cards.big: Does sedition law protect harmony or silence legitimate dissent?
Pakatan Harapan pledged to abolish the Sedition Act in 2018
